onboarding.title: Where Should I Start? (Personal Roadmap)
onboarding.desc: Select the challenge that bothers you the most right now; you will be directed straight to the steps that fit your brain chemistry best:

baslangic.title: Cannot Start Working
baslangic.desc: First momentum to break inertia and procrastination.

dikkat.title: Easily Distracted
dikkat.desc: Silencing environmental distractions and sustaining focus.

zihin.title: Mind is Too Cluttered
zihin.desc: Unloading the working memory load from the prefrontal cortex.

adhd.title: ADHD: Working Techniques + Neuroscientific Hacks

motivasyon.subject: Source of motivation
motivasyon.adhd: The engine does not start without interest, curiosity, urgency, or immediate reward.
motivasyon.normal: Can work by visualizing future rewards and staying committed to goals.

zaman.subject: Time perception
zaman.adhd: "Now" and "not now" — intermediate time perception is weak.
zaman.normal: Past, present, and future are perceived linearly and clearly.

dikkat.subject: Attention span
dikkat.adhd: Hyperfocus (2-4 hours non-stop) or zero focus — there is no middle ground.

calisma.subject: Best working block
calisma.adhd: Short and intense sprints (15-25 mins), followed by active physical breaks.
calisma.normal: Long and stable study blocks (45-90 mins) provide high efficiency.

yapi.subject: Need for structure
yapi.adhd: External structures are required (alarms, checklists, body double, etc.).
yapi.normal: Internal discipline and plans are sufficient; external support is optional.

gurultu.subject: Noise / environment
gurultu.adhd: Background rhythms (lofi, cafe ambient noise) support focus.
gurultu.normal: Absolute silence or light, melody-free instrumental sounds are preferred.

baslama.subject: Task initiation
baslama.adhd: Starting is many times harder than finishing — the first step is the greatest resistance.
baslama.normal: Starting a task is relatively easy, procrastination loops are shorter.

adhd.desc: A comprehensive practical guide designed to understand the neurological structure of the ADHD brain, optimize the dopamine mechanism, and relieve the load on the prefrontal cortex.
adhd.crossDesc: While the ADHD brain seeks stimulation and flexibility, the OCD brain develops loops of control and certainty. To compare these two opposite neurological structures, review our OCD page.
adhd.healthWarning: Important Health Warning: Nutritional supplements should be used under physician supervision and when deficiencies are detected in blood tests. They do not replace medication.
adhd.noHacksFound: No hacks matching this category and filter combination were found. Please change the filter settings.
adhd.cognitiveHintDesc: In the ADHD brain, the core issue is not a lack of focus, but difficulty regulating dopamine levels. Techniques should help cross this chemical threshold.
adhd.neurotypicalHintDesc: The Neurotypical Brain can gain motivation from long-term goals and future visions. The main trap: over-planning and delaying execution.
adhd.universalHintDesc: These study principles directly enhance cognitive performance and memory consolidation in all individuals, neurotypical or ADHD alike.

okb.title: OCD: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder Guide
okb.description: Strategies for breaking obsession and compulsion loops, calming the brain's uncertainty alarm, and implementing ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) protocols.
okb.connectionDesc: While the ADHD brain struggles to initiate focus due to dopamine deficits, the OCD brain hyper-focuses and gets stuck due to hyper-active error signaling.
okb.loopObsessionDesc: Unwanted, sudden intrusive thoughts, doubts, or distressing fears.
okb.loopObsessionExample: E.g.: "What if I didn't lock the door?"
okb.loopAnxietyDesc: The amygdala sounds an emergency alarm, creating intense anxiety and distress.
okb.loopCompulsionDesc: Repetitive physical or mental actions taken to suppress the spike in anxiety.
okb.loopCompulsionExample: E.g.: Going back and checking the lock repeatedly
okb.loopReliefDesc: Anxiety drops temporarily, but this action reinforces to the brain that the compulsion was necessary.
okb.erpGoldenRuleDesc: When you block the compulsion, anxiety does not increase infinitely. Eventually, the brain naturally de-escalates the alarm (Habituation).
okb.erpCard1Desc: Map your triggers from 1 to 10. Begin response prevention exercises with the lowest rating to safely build momentum.
okb.erpCard2Desc: When triggered, wait 5 minutes before carrying out a compulsion. Gradually increase this delay to 15, 30, and eventually 60 minutes.
okb.erpCard3Desc: Accept that 100% certainty is a neurobiological illusion. Remind your brain: "Maybe it is, maybe it isn't. I will tolerate this risk."

polimatlik.title: Polymathy: Multidisciplinary Mastery
polimatlik.description: Synthesis methods for connecting science and art fields, building T-shaped skillset profiles, and managing wide-ranging learning curiosities.
polimatlik.connectionDesc: The ADHD brain's rapid shift in interests and divergent thinking style, when synthesized correctly, becomes a highly creative engine for polymathy.
polimatlik.tshapedHorizontalDesc: Acquiring enough working vocabulary to form connections, cross-pollinate ideas, and collaborate with niche subject experts.
polimatlik.tshapedHorizontalExample: E.g.: Basic understanding of Coding, Psychology, Design, and Finance.
polimatlik.tshapedVerticalSubtitle: World-class expertise in one core domain
polimatlik.tshapedVerticalDesc: Building deep specialization in at least one field to drive value production, authority, and maintain solid professional footing.
polimatlik.tshapedVerticalExample: E.g.: Senior Software Architect or Medical Doctor.
polimatlik.synthesisGoldenRuleDesc: Creative leaps occur when you transplant a mental model from one domain into an entirely foreign subject.
polimatlik.synthesisCard1Desc: Instead of purely absorbing theory, build concrete projects that merge fields. Example: Coding an interactive history database.
polimatlik.synthesisCard2Desc: Avoid diluting energy across dozens of targets. Dedicate 3-month sprints to master one specific secondary domain at a time.
polimatlik.synthesisCard3Desc: Spend 10 minutes looking for structural similarities between two unrelated concepts you learned today, writing down analogies.

footer.vision: To translate complex academic research and neurodevelopmental findings into practical, actionable cognitive protocols for everyone, scientifically maximizing mental potential and quality of life.
footer.mission: To aggregate evidence-based, ethical, and sustainable learning methodologies that cater to the unique cognitive profiles of neurodivergent brains (ADHD, OCD) and multi-disciplinary learners (Polymathy) in an open-source framework.
